critical.mandate: Architecture must exist before creating epics
Every story must have depends_on and blocks declarations
Stories must be ordered by dependency topology + business priority

step.title: Define Epics
step.action: Group related features into logical epics
Each epic: name, description, goal, success criteria

step.title: Break Into Stories
step.action: For each epic, create user stories
Each story needs: title, description, acceptance criteria, size estimate (S/M/L/XL)
Use format: "As a [user], I want to [action] so that [benefit]"

step.title: Apply Test-Plan Risk Levels
step.action: Read {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md — extract risk assessment: high-risk areas (revenue-critical, security-sensitive, complex logic)
For each story: if it touches a high-risk component, set risk_level: high. Otherwise medium or low.
High-risk stories: add to Dev Notes: "Risk: HIGH — run /gaia-atdd before /gaia-dev-story"

step.title: Declare Dependencies
step.action: For each story, declare depends_on: [story-ids] and blocks: [story-ids]

step.title: Priority Ordering
step.action: Sort stories by: dependency topology first, then business priority

step.title: Generate Output
Optional: Edge Case Analysis
step.ask: Would you like to hunt for edge cases in the stories? /gaia-edge-cases will identify boundary conditions, race conditions, null/empty inputs, overflow scenarios, and error chains. Recommended to catch gaps before sprint planning. (yes/no)
step.action: If no: skip — edge case analysis can be run anytime later with /gaia-edge-cases

step.title: Optional: Adversarial Review
step.ask: Would you like to run an adversarial review on the epics and stories? This will challenge story completeness, missing edge cases, dependency gaps, and acceptance criteria quality. Recommended before sprint planning. (yes/no)
step.action: If no: skip — adversarial review can be run anytime later with /gaia-adversarial
